# Texture System

> Texture loading, filtering, and addressing modes

**Category:** Integration · **Version:** 1.0.0 · **Tags:** integration · godot

## What this does

Texture loading, filtering, and addressing modes

Specifies 5 acceptance outcomes that any implementation must satisfy, regardless of language or framework.

## What must be true

- **implementation:** Feature implemented in C++ engine core
- **platform_agnostic:** Works across desktop, web, mobile platforms

## Success & failure scenarios

**✅ Success paths**

- **Texture Creation** — when Create from image data is requested, then Create from image data completed.
- **Texture Loading** — when Load PNG, JPG, WebP, etc. is requested, then Load PNG, JPG, WebP, etc. completed.
- **Texture Filtering** — when Linear, nearest, anisotropic is requested, then Linear, nearest, anisotropic completed.
- **Texture Addressing** — when Wrap, clamp, mirror modes is requested, then Wrap, clamp, mirror modes completed.
- **Texture Atlasing** — when Pack multiple textures is requested, then Pack multiple textures completed.
